feedback.text: Currently the games industry is setting a precedent of just removing access to purchased products from the individual through use of data held on servers that must be accessed to make the product function. While a luxury and entertainment product this can only be seen as precedent for industries to do the same with digital products as a whole declaring purchased product a "Licence" (the current line adopted by the company Ubisoft with regard to their discontinuing access to games like 'The Crew') to use the product post-facto but with an undefined end point and no warning that they will decide when the "Licence" expires even with physical media. In this manner creating another type of planned obsolescence that consumers must contend with, a majority of whom will not even be aware of, when making purchases. The only outcome will be an acceleration of the current model where the companies will just abandon older iterations to force adopting new devices, the current model favoured by companies like Apple, so consumers are not left either wholly unprotected or risking fixes in the public/private sphere involving third parties. Another contentious issue is the current choice of Payment Processors to decide what I can and cannot view and purchase online at the behest of a series of fundamentalists based in Australia who are seeking large scale media censorship by using "Adult" titles on games Store fronts like Steam and Itch.io as the vector for creating a wedge to continuously expand the definition of Adult and Pornographic. This is already being used as a pretence on websites like Twitter/X to censor news feeds that for its own benefit or the benefit of those it affiliates. They are at the same time declaring the existence of LGBTQIA+ people as being pornographic in an effort to further the hatespeech agenda of dangerous groups using religion as their shield against criticism for their goal to control the lives of others in whatever ways they can. These are simple enough reasons to reject things in a secular free society that does not obey any religious leader as that will only breed greater danger to all liberties, races and creeds.
